title: rinorea auriculata
intro: Rinorea auriculata, also known as the ear-leaved false lupine, is a plant species belonging to the family Violaceae. It is traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antimalarial and antipyretic effects. The health benefits of Rinorea auriculata include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating malaria, with some studies suggesting its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties as well. In herbal preparations, Rinorea auriculata is often used in infusions, decoctions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although its use should be guided by a healthcare professional due to potential side effects and interactions.
